verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation; it has no TM domain or extracellular domain. Under baseline conditions the protein body faces the cytoplasm. However, a documented noncanonical pathway — autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE) — topologically inverts Src onto the outer face of the plasma membrane specifically in cancer cells, enabling antibody-based therapeutic targeting. This is a cell-state/cancer-state-induced surface exposure of the protein body itself via lysosomal exocytosis, qualifying as contextual surface accessibility.
